meta.title: Bifuka Ski Area
meta.description: Northern Hokkaido dry powder at ¥70 per lift ride—FIS aerial site and half-day gastro micro-tourism with craft beer and hot springs.

hero.title:
Bifuka
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Dry powder and ¥70 lift rides—a tiny municipal hill where you own the untracked lines.

sanctuary.title:
Hidden from Nayoro—
own the untracked.
sanctuary.lead: One single lift, ~141 m vertical—a compact town-run hill with inland dry powder and wide intermediate pitches. Reviews praise carving practice conditions; zero crowds make it a powder hunter's secret.
sanctuary.noticeBody: "Nobody stays all day"—don't hide the scale; design for a focused morning ski plus afternoon gastro loop.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Night-lit Bifuka slope—quiet runs overlooking town lights

primary.title: ¥70 × dry powder
primary.lead: Adult single ride ¥70, 11-ride ticket ¥700—among Japan's cheapest. Inland cold creates low-moisture snow; hidden from Nayoro Piyashiri, lines stay fresh. A secret carving gym for locals in the know.
primary.imgAlt: Untouched dry powder—carving wide turns on Bifuka's compact bowl

secondary.title: FIS aerial site
secondary.lead: International-level jump on a tiny municipal hill—built in 2005 on Bifuka's trampoline tradition. No public skiing on the jump, but a unique landmark and event draw.

pass.title:
After skiing,
eat the whole town.
pass.lead: Bundle 11 lift rides (¥700), Matatabi Curry lunch, Bifuka Onsen day entry, and Restaurant BSB craft beer—turn a ¥700 ski stop into a ¥20,000 half-day area journey.
pass.imgAlt: Bifuka red-brick warehouse—Restaurant BSB craft brewery

hike.title:
Spice curry &
natural hot springs.
hike.lead: No on-hill restaurant—high-quality town eateries fill the gap. Matatabi Curry's Taiwanese mince topping and ¥500 day-use Bifuka Onsen sauna are the classic après loop.
hike.leadCurry: Lunch at Matatabi Curry, dinner at Niku BAR Minami lamb or Bibliothéque wood-fired pizza—a town of 4,000 with surprising food density.
hike.imgAlt: Bifuka spice curry—popular shop run by transplanted chefs

tour.title:
Northern powder,
done in half a day.
tour.lead: For powder hunters and gastro travelers—2–3 hours of focused laps, then a town food-and-bath circuit.

sanctuary.title: Powder hunter guide
sanctuary.body: Vs Nayoro Piyashiri (13 courses, 2,000 m) you lose on scale—you win on price and privacy. Long-term play: pitch "unknown local JAPOW" to international hunters avoiding Niseko crowds.

workation.title: Private hill rental concept
workation.body: Flip ¥70 rides into hourly hill buyouts—tiny size becomes exclusive private powder for affluent guests at premium pricing.

lake.title: FIS aerial camp vision
lake.body: 2005 Bifuka Aerial Project—winter sport built on trampoline town heritage. Training camps and spectator tours as a dual athlete-tourism pipeline.

access.note: ~53 km from Shibetsu-Kembuchi IC via Route 40 · ~1.5 hr from Asahikawa · ~3 hr from Sapporo · free parking
access.phoneOps: Bifuka Board of Education, sports division
access.rentacarEyebrow: 道北ドライパウダーと70円リフト——クラフトビールと秘湯で完結する半日マイクロツーリズム。
